---
title: "GWAS allele frequencies in sub populations"
author: "Elizabeth Campbell"
date: "2025-12-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# ================================================================================
# ALLELE FREQUENCY & GEOGRAPHIC CORRELATION ANALYSIS - UPDATED VERSION
# For GWAS Presentation - Climate Adaptation in Kernza
# ================================================================================
# Author: Analysis for Elizabeth's Kernza climate adaptation research
# Updated to work with actual file locations and structures
# ================================================================================

cat("\n")
cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("ALLELE FREQUENCY & GEOGRAPHIC CORRELATION ANALYSIS\n")
cat("Climate Adaptation in Intermediate Wheatgrass (Kernza)\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# LOAD REQUIRED PACKAGES
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 1: Loading required packages...\n")
required_packages <- c("data.table", "ggplot2", "dplyr", "tidyr", "viridis", 
                       "gridExtra", "vcfR", "readxl")

for (pkg in required_packages) {
  if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE)) {
    cat("  Installing", pkg, "...\n")
    install.packages(pkg, repos = "http://cran.us.r-project.org")
    library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
  }
}
cat("✓ All packages loaded successfully\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# FILE PATHS
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 2: Setting up file paths...\n")

BASE_DIR <- "C:/Users/Lizzy/OneDrive/Documents/UH_Manoa_Classes/IWG_Chapter_2"
PHENO_DIR <- file.path(BASE_DIR, "Phenotype_and_Ecographic_Data")
GENO_DIR <- file.path(BASE_DIR, "Raw_genotype_data")
GWAS_DIR <- file.path(BASE_DIR, "Analysis_Outputs_and_Visualizations", "GWAS")
POP_DIR <- file.path(BASE_DIR, "Analysis_Outputs_and_Visualizations", "Hierarchical Clusters")
OUTPUT_DIR <- file.path(BASE_DIR, "Analysis_Outputs_and_Visualizations")

# Create output subdirectory
AF_OUTPUT_DIR <- file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Allele_Frequency_Analysis")
if (!dir.exists(AF_OUTPUT_DIR)) {
  dir.create(AF_OUTPUT_DIR, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("✓ Created output directory:", AF_OUTPUT_DIR, "\n")
}

cat("✓ File paths configured\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# STEP 3: LOAD POPULATION STRUCTURE
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 3: Loading population structure data...\n")

# Load the K=6 cluster assignments
pop_file <- file.path(POP_DIR, "IWG_pca_clusters_k6.csv")
if (!file.exists(pop_file)) {
  cat("ERROR: Population file not found at:", pop_file, "\n")
  cat("Please check the file path!\n")
  stop("Missing population file")
}

pop_data <- fread(file = pop_file, header = TRUE)
cat("  ✓ Loaded", nrow(pop_data), "samples with population assignments\n")
cat("  Columns:", paste(colnames(pop_data), collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Show population distribution
cat("\n  Population distribution (K=6 clusters):\n")
pop_table <- table(pop_data$Cluster)
for (clust in names(pop_table)) {
  cat(sprintf("    Cluster %s: %d samples\n", clust, pop_table[clust]))
}
cat("\n")

# ================================================================================
# STEP 4: LOAD CLIMATE DATA
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 4: Loading climate (bioclim) data...\n")

climate_file <- file.path(PHENO_DIR, "IWG_bioclim.csv")
if (!file.exists(climate_file)) {
  cat("ERROR: Climate file not found at:", climate_file, "\n")
  stop("Missing climate file")
}

climate_data <- fread(file = climate_file, header = TRUE)
cat("  ✓ Loaded climate data for", nrow(climate_data), "accessions\n")
cat("  Climate variables available:", sum(grepl("wc2.1", colnames(climate_data))), "\n")

# Rename climate columns for easier use
# BIO1 = Annual Mean Temperature, BIO12 = Annual Precipitation
colnames(climate_data)[colnames(climate_data) == "wc2.1_30s_bio_1"] <- "BIO1"
colnames(climate_data)[colnames(climate_data) == "wc2.1_30s_bio_12"] <- "BIO12"

# Remove rows with missing climate data
climate_data_clean <- climate_data[!is.na(climate_data$BIO1) & !is.na(climate_data$BIO12), ]
cat("  ✓ Retained", nrow(climate_data_clean), "samples with complete climate data\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# STEP 5: MERGE POPULATION AND CLIMATE DATA
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 5: Merging population and climate data...\n")

merged_data <- merge(pop_data, climate_data_clean,
                     by.x = "Accession",
                     by.y = "PI_Accession",
                     all = FALSE)

cat("  ✓ Merged dataset has", nrow(merged_data), "samples\n")
cat("  Final columns:", paste(colnames(merged_data)[1:10], collapse = ", "), "...\n\n")

# Summary statistics by population
cat("  Climate summary by population:\n")
climate_summary <- merged_data %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_temp = round(mean(BIO1, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    sd_temp = round(sd(BIO1, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    mean_precip = round(mean(BIO12, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    sd_precip = round(sd(BIO12, na.rm = TRUE), 0)
  )
print(climate_summary)
cat("\n")

# ================================================================================
# STEP 6: LOAD GWAS RESULTS
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 6: Loading GWAS results...\n")
cat("  Searching for GWAS result files in:", GWAS_DIR, "\n")

# List all GWAS files
gwas_files <- list.files(GWAS_DIR, pattern = "gwas_four_models_bio", full.names = TRUE)
cat("  Found", length(gwas_files), "GWAS result files\n")

if (length(gwas_files) == 0) {
  cat("  WARNING: No GWAS files found. Please check the directory.\n")
  cat("  Looking for files matching pattern: gwas_four_models_bio*\n")
  cat("  Available files in GWAS directory:\n")
  print(list.files(GWAS_DIR))
}

# For this analysis, let's focus on BIO1 (temperature) and BIO12 (precipitation)
# You can modify this to include other traits
gwas_bio1_file <- file.path(GWAS_DIR, "gwas_four_models_bio1.csv")
gwas_bio12_file <- file.path(GWAS_DIR, "gwas_four_models_bio12.csv")

# Load GWAS results
all_gwas_results <- data.frame()

for (gwas_file in c(gwas_bio1_file, gwas_bio12_file)) {
  if (file.exists(gwas_file)) {
    trait_name <- gsub(".*bio([0-9]+).*", "BIO\\1", basename(gwas_file))
    gwas_temp <- fread(file = gwas_file, header = TRUE)
    gwas_temp$Trait <- trait_name
    
    # Show what models are in the data
    if("model" %in% colnames(gwas_temp)) {
      cat("    Models found:", paste(unique(gwas_temp$model), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    }
    
    all_gwas_results <- rbind(all_gwas_results, gwas_temp)
    cat("  ✓ Loaded", nrow(gwas_temp), "SNPs for", trait_name, "\n")
  } else {
    cat("  WARNING: File not found:", gwas_file, "\n")
  }
}

if (nrow(all_gwas_results) == 0) {
  cat("\nERROR: No GWAS results loaded. Please check file paths and names.\n")
  cat("Expected files:\n")
  cat("  -", gwas_bio1_file, "\n")
  cat("  -", gwas_bio12_file, "\n")
  stop("Cannot proceed without GWAS results")
}

cat("\n  Total GWAS results loaded:", nrow(all_gwas_results), "SNPs across traits\n")
cat("  Columns:", paste(colnames(all_gwas_results)[1:10], collapse = ", "), "...\n\n")

# Rename marker column to SNP for consistency
if("marker" %in% colnames(all_gwas_results)) {
  colnames(all_gwas_results)[colnames(all_gwas_results) == "marker"] <- "SNP"
  cat("  ✓ Renamed 'marker' column to 'SNP'\n")
}

# Rename p_value column to p for consistency
if("p_value" %in% colnames(all_gwas_results)) {
  colnames(all_gwas_results)[colnames(all_gwas_results) == "p_value"] <- "p"
  cat("  ✓ Renamed 'p_value' column to 'p'\n\n")
}

# If multiple models are present, you can filter for one specific model
# Uncomment and modify the line below if you want to use only one model:
# all_gwas_results <- all_gwas_results[all_gwas_results$model == "Q+K", ]

# Show model distribution if applicable
if("model" %in% colnames(all_gwas_results)) {
  cat("  Model distribution in GWAS results:\n")
  model_table <- table(all_gwas_results$model)
  for(m in names(model_table)) {
    cat(sprintf("    %s: %d SNPs\n", m, model_table[m]))
  }
  cat("\n")
}

# Filter for significant SNPs (adjust p-value threshold as needed)
# Common thresholds: 0.05, 1e-5, 1e-6, or Bonferroni correction
p_threshold <- 1e-5  # MODIFY THIS IF NEEDED
sig_gwas <- all_gwas_results[all_gwas_results$p < p_threshold, ]
cat("  Significant SNPs (p <", p_threshold, "):", nrow(sig_gwas), "\n\n")

if (nrow(sig_gwas) == 0) {
  cat("  WARNING: No SNPs meet significance threshold. Trying p < 0.01...\n")
  p_threshold <- 0.01
  sig_gwas <- all_gwas_results[all_gwas_results$p < p_threshold, ]
  cat("  Significant SNPs (p <", p_threshold, "):", nrow(sig_gwas), "\n\n")
}

# If we have multiple models, keep the best p-value for each SNP
if("model" %in% colnames(sig_gwas) && length(unique(sig_gwas$model)) > 1) {
  cat("  Multiple models detected - keeping best p-value for each SNP...\n")
  n_before <- nrow(sig_gwas)
  
  # For each SNP, keep the row with the smallest p-value
  sig_gwas <- sig_gwas %>%
    group_by(SNP, Trait) %>%
    arrange(p) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    as.data.frame()
  
  n_after <- nrow(sig_gwas)
  cat("  Reduced from", n_before, "to", n_after, "unique SNPs\n\n")
}

# ================================================================================
# STEP 7: LOAD GENOTYPE DATA
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 7: Loading genotype data...\n")

vcf_file <- file.path(GENO_DIR, "IWG_PI.vcf")

if (!file.exists(vcf_file)) {
  cat("ERROR: VCF file not found at:", vcf_file, "\n")
  stop("Missing genotype file")
}

cat("  Reading VCF file (this may take a few minutes)...\n")
vcf <- read.vcfR(vcf_file, verbose = FALSE)
cat("  ✓ VCF loaded:", nrow(vcf), "SNPs,", ncol(vcf@gt) - 1, "samples\n")

# Extract genotype matrix
cat("  Extracting genotype matrix...\n")
gt_matrix <- extract.gt(vcf, element = "GT")

# Convert genotypes to numeric (0, 1, 2)
cat("  Converting genotypes to numeric format...\n")
convert_gt <- function(gt) {
  # Convert VCF genotypes to dosage format
  # 0/0 or 0|0 -> 0
  # 0/1 or 1/0 or 0|1 or 1|0 -> 1
  # 1/1 or 1|1 -> 2
  # ./. or .|. -> NA
  gt <- gsub("\\|", "/", gt)  # Convert | to /
  gt <- ifelse(gt == "0/0", 0,
               ifelse(gt == "1/0" | gt == "0/1", 1,
                      ifelse(gt == "1/1", 2, NA)))
  return(as.numeric(gt))
}

gt_numeric <- apply(gt_matrix, 2, convert_gt)
rownames(gt_numeric) <- paste0("S", vcf@fix[, "CHROM"], "_", vcf@fix[, "POS"])

cat("  ✓ Converted to numeric format:", nrow(gt_numeric), "SNPs\n")
cat("  Sample names:", paste(colnames(gt_numeric)[1:5], collapse = ", "), "...\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# STEP 8: MATCH SAMPLES ACROSS DATASETS
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 8: Matching samples across datasets...\n")

# Get common samples
geno_samples <- colnames(gt_numeric)
pop_samples <- merged_data$Accession
common_samples <- intersect(geno_samples, pop_samples)

cat("  Genotype samples:", length(geno_samples), "\n")
cat("  Population/climate samples:", length(pop_samples), "\n")
cat("  Common samples:", length(common_samples), "\n\n")

if (length(common_samples) < 50) {
  cat("  WARNING: Few samples overlap. Checking for naming differences...\n")
  cat("  First 10 genotype sample names:", paste(head(geno_samples, 10), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("  First 10 population sample names:", paste(head(pop_samples, 10), collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

# Subset data to common samples
gt_numeric_subset <- gt_numeric[, common_samples]
merged_data_subset <- merged_data[merged_data$Accession %in% common_samples, ]

cat("  ✓ Using", length(common_samples), "samples for analysis\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# STEP 9: CALCULATE ALLELE FREQUENCIES
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 9: Calculating allele frequencies by population...\n")

# Match SNP IDs between GWAS results and genotype data
gwas_snp_ids <- sig_gwas$SNP
geno_snp_ids <- rownames(gt_numeric_subset)

# Try to match SNPs (may need adjustment based on naming conventions)
matched_snps <- intersect(gwas_snp_ids, geno_snp_ids)
cat("  GWAS significant SNPs:", length(gwas_snp_ids), "\n")
cat("  Genotype SNPs:", length(geno_snp_ids), "\n")
cat("  Matched SNPs:", length(matched_snps), "\n\n")

# Create SNP ID mapping table for direct matches
if (length(matched_snps) > 0) {
  cat("  ✓ Direct matching successful!\n")
  snp_id_map <- data.frame(
    gwas_id = matched_snps,
    geno_id = matched_snps,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

if (length(matched_snps) == 0) {
  cat("  WARNING: No SNP ID matches between GWAS and genotype data!\n")
  cat("  First 5 GWAS SNP IDs:", paste(head(gwas_snp_ids, 5), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("  First 5 Genotype SNP IDs:", paste(head(geno_snp_ids, 5), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("\n  Attempting to match by position...\n")
  
  # Try alternative matching strategies here if needed
  
  # Strategy 1: Try matching just the position part
  cat("  Attempting to match by chromosome and position...\n")
  
  # Try multiple matching strategies
  
  # Strategy 1: Extract chromosome and position from genotype IDs (format: SJ01_14053676)
  geno_df <- data.frame(
    geno_id = geno_snp_ids,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  geno_df$chr <- sapply(strsplit(geno_df$geno_id, "_"), function(x) gsub("^S", "", x[1]))
  geno_df$pos <- sapply(strsplit(geno_df$geno_id, "_"), function(x) x[2])
  
  # Strategy 2: Check GWAS SNP ID format
  cat("  Checking GWAS SNP ID format...\n")
  cat("  First 10 GWAS SNPs:", paste(head(gwas_snp_ids, 10), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Try to extract chr and pos from GWAS IDs
  # Common formats: "S1_12345", "chr1_12345", "1_12345", "rs123456"
  gwas_df <- data.frame(
    gwas_id = gwas_snp_ids,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Check if GWAS IDs contain underscores (chr_pos format)
  if(any(grepl("_", gwas_df$gwas_id))) {
    gwas_df$chr <- sapply(strsplit(as.character(gwas_df$gwas_id), "_"), function(x) {
      chr <- x[1]
      chr <- gsub("^S", "", chr)  # Remove leading S if present
      chr <- gsub("^chr", "", chr)  # Remove "chr" prefix if present
      return(chr)
    })
    gwas_df$pos <- sapply(strsplit(as.character(gwas_df$gwas_id), "_"), function(x) x[2])
  } else {
    cat("  GWAS SNPs don't have underscore format. Need different matching strategy.\n")
    gwas_df$chr <- NA
    gwas_df$pos <- NA
  }
  
  # Try matching by chr and pos
  geno_df$chr_pos <- paste(geno_df$chr, geno_df$pos, sep="_")
  gwas_df$chr_pos <- paste(gwas_df$chr, gwas_df$pos, sep="_")
  
  matched_chr_pos <- intersect(gwas_df$chr_pos[!is.na(gwas_df$chr_pos)], 
                               geno_df$chr_pos[!is.na(geno_df$chr_pos)])
  
  if(length(matched_chr_pos) > 0) {
    cat("  ✓ Found", length(matched_chr_pos), "matches by chr:pos!\n\n")
    
    # Get the genotype IDs for matched positions
    matched_snps <- geno_df$geno_id[geno_df$chr_pos %in% matched_chr_pos]
    
    # Also store the GWAS IDs for annotation
    gwas_match_table <- gwas_df[gwas_df$chr_pos %in% matched_chr_pos, c("gwas_id", "chr_pos")]
    geno_match_table <- geno_df[geno_df$chr_pos %in% matched_chr_pos, c("geno_id", "chr_pos")]
    snp_id_map <- merge(gwas_match_table, geno_match_table, by = "chr_pos")
    
  } else {
    cat("\n")
    cat("================================================================================\n")
    cat("Still no matches found!\n")
    cat("================================================================================\n")
    cat("\nDebugging information:\n")
    cat("\nGWAS SNP format examples:\n")
    print(head(gwas_df, 10))
    cat("\nGenotype SNP format examples:\n")
    print(head(geno_df, 10))
    cat("\nThe SNP ID formats don't match. You may need to:\n")
    cat("1. Check the column name for SNP IDs in your GWAS file\n")
    cat("2. Modify the matching logic based on your specific format\n")
    cat("3. Pre-process one of the files to match the other's format\n")
    stop("Cannot match SNPs between datasets")
  }
}

# Calculate allele frequencies
allele_freq_results <- data.frame()

if(length(matched_snps) == 0) {
  cat("\n")
  cat("================================================================================\n")
  cat("ERROR: Cannot proceed - no SNPs matched!\n")
  cat("================================================================================\n")
  cat("\nDebugging information:\n")
  cat("\nExample GWAS SNP IDs:\n")
  print(head(gwas_snp_ids, 20))
  cat("\nExample Genotype SNP IDs:\n")
  print(head(geno_snp_ids, 20))
  cat("\nPlease check the SNP ID format and update the matching logic if needed.\n")
  cat("You may need to modify the script to match your specific SNP naming convention.\n")
  stop("No SNP matches found between GWAS and genotype data")
}

populations <- unique(merged_data_subset$Cluster)
cat("  Calculating for", length(populations), "populations\n")
cat("  Progress: ")

for (i in seq_along(matched_snps)) {
  if (i %% 50 == 0) cat(".")
  
  snp <- matched_snps[i]
  snp_geno <- gt_numeric_subset[snp, ]
  
  # Get GWAS info for this SNP using the mapping table
  geno_id_match <- snp_id_map$geno_id[snp_id_map$geno_id == snp]
  if(length(geno_id_match) > 0) {
    gwas_id_match <- snp_id_map$gwas_id[snp_id_map$geno_id == snp][1]
    snp_gwas_info <- sig_gwas[sig_gwas$SNP == gwas_id_match, ][1, ]
  } else {
    # Fallback - try direct match
    snp_gwas_info <- sig_gwas[sig_gwas$SNP == snp, ][1, ]
  }
  
  for (pop in populations) {
    # Get samples in this population
    pop_sample_ids <- merged_data_subset$Accession[merged_data_subset$Cluster == pop]
    
    # Get genotypes for these samples
    pop_genotypes <- snp_geno[pop_sample_ids]
    
    # Calculate allele frequency (proportion of alternative alleles)
    n_samples <- sum(!is.na(pop_genotypes))
    if (n_samples > 0) {
      alt_allele_freq <- sum(pop_genotypes, na.rm = TRUE) / (2 * n_samples)
      
      # Get mean climate for this population
      pop_climate <- merged_data_subset[merged_data_subset$Cluster == pop, ]
      mean_temp <- mean(pop_climate$BIO1, na.rm = TRUE)
      mean_precip <- mean(pop_climate$BIO12, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Store result
      allele_freq_results <- rbind(allele_freq_results, data.frame(
        SNP = snp,
        Population = pop,
        Allele_Freq = alt_allele_freq,
        n_samples = n_samples,
        Trait = snp_gwas_info$Trait,
        P_value = snp_gwas_info$p,
        Effect = if("effect" %in% colnames(snp_gwas_info)) snp_gwas_info$effect else NA,
        Model = if("model" %in% colnames(snp_gwas_info)) snp_gwas_info$model else NA,
        Mean_BIO1 = mean_temp,
        Mean_BIO12 = mean_precip,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
}

cat(" Done!\n")
cat("  ✓ Calculated frequencies for", length(unique(allele_freq_results$SNP)), "SNPs\n\n")

# Check if we have any data
if(nrow(allele_freq_results) == 0) {
  cat("\n")
  cat("================================================================================\n")
  cat("ERROR: No allele frequencies calculated!\n")
  cat("================================================================================\n")
  cat("This means SNP matching failed. Check the debugging output above.\n")
  stop("Cannot proceed without allele frequency data")
}

# Save allele frequency results
af_file <- file.path(AF_OUTPUT_DIR, "allele_frequencies_by_population.csv")
fwrite(allele_freq_results, af_file)
cat("  ✓ Saved to:", af_file, "\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# STEP 10: TEST GEOGRAPHIC CORRELATIONS
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 10: Testing correlations with climate...\n")

correlation_results <- data.frame()

for (snp in unique(allele_freq_results$SNP)) {
  snp_data <- allele_freq_results[allele_freq_results$SNP == snp, ]
  
  # Correlation with temperature
  if (length(unique(snp_data$Mean_BIO1)) > 1) {
    cor_temp <- cor.test(snp_data$Allele_Freq, snp_data$Mean_BIO1, method = "pearson")
    r_temp <- cor_temp$estimate
    p_temp <- cor_temp$p.value
  } else {
    r_temp <- NA
    p_temp <- NA
  }
  
  # Correlation with precipitation
  if (length(unique(snp_data$Mean_BIO12)) > 1) {
    cor_precip <- cor.test(snp_data$Allele_Freq, snp_data$Mean_BIO12, method = "pearson")
    r_precip <- cor_precip$estimate
    p_precip <- cor_precip$p.value
  } else {
    r_precip <- NA
    p_precip <- NA
  }
  
  correlation_results <- rbind(correlation_results, data.frame(
    SNP = snp,
    Trait = snp_data$Trait[1],
    P_value_GWAS = snp_data$P_value[1],
    Effect = snp_data$Effect[1],
    Model = snp_data$Model[1],
    Cor_Temperature = r_temp,
    P_Temperature = p_temp,
    Cor_Precipitation = r_precip,
    P_Precipitation = p_precip,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ))
}

# Save correlation results
cor_file <- file.path(AF_OUTPUT_DIR, "climate_correlations.csv")
fwrite(correlation_results, cor_file)
cat("  ✓ Saved to:", cor_file, "\n\n")

# Print summary
sig_temp_cor <- correlation_results[!is.na(correlation_results$P_Temperature) & 
                                    correlation_results$P_Temperature < 0.05, ]
sig_precip_cor <- correlation_results[!is.na(correlation_results$P_Precipitation) & 
                                      correlation_results$P_Precipitation < 0.05, ]

cat("  Significant temperature correlations (p < 0.05):", nrow(sig_temp_cor), "\n")
cat("  Significant precipitation correlations (p < 0.05):", nrow(sig_precip_cor), "\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# STEP 11: CREATE VISUALIZATIONS
# ================================================================================

cat("Step 11: Creating visualizations...\n\n")

# Convert Population to factor for plotting
allele_freq_results$Population <- as.factor(allele_freq_results$Population)

# --- VISUALIZATION 1: HEATMAP ---
cat("  Creating allele frequency heatmap...\n")

# Limit to SNPs with interesting patterns (variable across populations)
af_variance <- allele_freq_results %>%
  group_by(SNP) %>%
  summarise(var = var(Allele_Freq, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(var))

top_variable_snps <- head(af_variance$SNP, min(50, nrow(af_variance)))
heatmap_data <- allele_freq_results[allele_freq_results$SNP %in% top_variable_snps, ]

p_heatmap <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = Population, y = SNP, fill = Allele_Freq)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis(option = "plasma", name = "Allele\nFrequency",
                     limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Allele Frequencies Across Populations",
       x = "Population (K=6 Cluster)",
       y = "SNP")

heatmap_file <- file.path(AF_OUTPUT_DIR, "allele_frequency_heatmap.png")
ggsave(heatmap_file, p_heatmap, width = 10, height = 12, dpi = 300)
cat("    ✓ Saved:", basename(heatmap_file), "\n")

# --- VISUALIZATION 2: TEMPERATURE CORRELATIONS ---
cat("  Creating temperature correlation plots...\n")

top_temp_snps <- correlation_results %>%
  filter(!is.na(Cor_Temperature)) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Cor_Temperature))) %>%
  head(9)

if (nrow(top_temp_snps) > 0) {
  plot_data_temp <- allele_freq_results %>%
    filter(SNP %in% top_temp_snps$SNP) %>%
    mutate(SNP_label = paste0(SNP, "\nr = ", 
                              round(correlation_results$Cor_Temperature[match(SNP, correlation_results$SNP)], 2)))
  
  p_temp <- ggplot(plot_data_temp, aes(x = Mean_BIO1, y = Allele_Freq, color = Population)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~SNP_label, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
    scale_color_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "turbo") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
          strip.background = element_rect(fill = "lightgray"),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +
    labs(title = "Allele Frequency vs Temperature",
         x = "Mean Annual Temperature (°C × 10)",
         y = "Alternative Allele Frequency",
         color = "Population")
  
  temp_file <- file.path(AF_OUTPUT_DIR, "allele_freq_vs_temperature.png")
  ggsave(temp_file, p_temp, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  cat("    ✓ Saved:", basename(temp_file), "\n")
}

# --- VISUALIZATION 3: PRECIPITATION CORRELATIONS ---
cat("  Creating precipitation correlation plots...\n")

top_precip_snps <- correlation_results %>%
  filter(!is.na(Cor_Precipitation)) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Cor_Precipitation))) %>%
  head(9)

if (nrow(top_precip_snps) > 0) {
  plot_data_precip <- allele_freq_results %>%
    filter(SNP %in% top_precip_snps$SNP) %>%
    mutate(SNP_label = paste0(SNP, "\nr = ", 
                              round(correlation_results$Cor_Precipitation[match(SNP, correlation_results$SNP)], 2)))
  
  p_precip <- ggplot(plot_data_precip, aes(x = Mean_BIO12, y = Allele_Freq, color = Population)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~SNP_label, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
    scale_color_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "turbo") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
          strip.background = element_rect(fill = "lightgray"),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +
    labs(title = "Allele Frequency vs Precipitation",
         x = "Annual Precipitation (mm)",
         y = "Alternative Allele Frequency",
         color = "Population")
  
  precip_file <- file.path(AF_OUTPUT_DIR, "allele_freq_vs_precipitation.png")
  ggsave(precip_file, p_precip, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  cat("    ✓ Saved:", basename(precip_file), "\n")
}

# --- VISUALIZATION 4: SUMMARY TABLE ---
cat("  Creating presentation summary table...\n")

summary_table <- correlation_results %>%
  arrange(P_value_GWAS) %>%
  head(20) %>%
  mutate(
    P_value_GWAS = format(P_value_GWAS, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2),
    Effect = round(Effect, 4),
    Cor_Temperature = round(Cor_Temperature, 3),
    P_Temperature = format(P_Temperature, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2),
    Cor_Precipitation = round(Cor_Precipitation, 3),
    P_Precipitation = format(P_Precipitation, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2)
  ) %>%
  select(SNP, Trait, P_value_GWAS, Effect, Model, Cor_Temperature, P_Temperature, 
         Cor_Precipitation, P_Precipitation)

summary_file <- file.path(AF_OUTPUT_DIR, "presentation_summary_table.csv")
fwrite(summary_table, summary_file)
cat("    ✓ Saved:", basename(summary_file), "\n\n")

# ================================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ================================================================================

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE!\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

cat("Output directory:", AF_OUTPUT_DIR, "\n\n")

cat("Files created:\n")
cat("  DATA FILES:\n")
cat("    1. allele_frequencies_by_population.csv\n")
cat("    2. climate_correlations.csv\n")
cat("    3. presentation_summary_table.csv\n\n")

cat("  FIGURES:\n")
cat("    1. allele_frequency_heatmap.png\n")
if (nrow(top_temp_snps) > 0) cat("    2. allele_freq_vs_temperature.png\n")
if (nrow(top_precip_snps) > 0) cat("    3. allele_freq_vs_precipitation.png\n")
cat("\n")

cat("Key Results:\n")
cat("  - SNPs analyzed:", length(unique(allele_freq_results$SNP)), "\n")
cat("  - Populations:", length(populations), "\n")
cat("  - Samples:", length(common_samples), "\n")
cat("  - Significant temperature correlations:", nrow(sig_temp_cor), "\n")
cat("  - Significant precipitation correlations:", nrow(sig_precip_cor), "\n\n")

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("Ready for your GWAS presentation!\n")
cat("Open the PNG files to view your figures.\n")
cat("================================================================================\n")
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
